title: 3G: Infrastructure-as-Code Deployment (CloudFormation/Terraform)
description: Modular IaC deployment supporting both AWS CloudFormation and Terraform. Single codebase with provider abstraction — user picks their tool. Covers: VPC/networking, ECS Fargate or Lambda for the seller agent, Redis (ElastiCache) for prod storage, SQS queues for event bus subscribers, API Gateway or ALB for the FastAPI endpoints, IAM roles, secrets management (SSM Parameter Store), CloudWatch logging. Structured as reusable modules: networking, compute, storage, messaging, api-gateway. Terraform modules and CloudFormation nested stacks follow the same logical structure so either can be used. Creates infra/ directory with terraform/ and cloudformation/ subdirectories. Phase 3 — post-pilot.

title: 1H: Media Kit & Public Inventory Discovery
description: Public-facing inventory catalog for buyer agents (authenticated or not). Three-layer package management: Layer 1 - sync packages from FreeWheel/GAM/SSP (admin sees what exists). Layer 2 - seller curation and promotion with wizard to create new package iterations (e.g. Winter Olympics Package, Peacock Premium CTV Bundle). Layer 3 - agent-assembled dynamic packages from existing placements/deal IDs. Public endpoint (no auth) returns packages, pricing ranges, demographics. Authenticated endpoint adds exact pricing, availability, performance. Natural language search + structured browsing. Creates src/ad_seller/models/media_kit.py. Modifies api/main.py, discovery_inquiry_flow.py. Depends on 1D for Layer 1 ad server sync.

title: 2B: Enhanced Negotiation Engine
description: Upgrade from single-round 70%% gap-split counter-proposal to competitive multi-round negotiation engine. Features: multi-round with concession tracking and strategy memory, dynamic pricing (match/exceed Prebid percentile-based approach), strategy per buyer tier (aggressive for unknown, collaborative for strategic), concession limits (max per round, total cap, walk-away), package-aware negotiation (counter with alternative packages not just price). Builds on counter.py, yield_optimizer.py, pricing_rules_engine.py. Depends on 1F (sessions), 1H (packages).

title: 2D: IAB Deals API v1.0 Integration
description: Implement IAB Tech Lab Deal Sync API v1.0 — standardized one-way push from seller (SSP) to buyer (DSP) for deal term transmission. Deal push endpoint (HTTP POST to DSP), deal status query (poll buyer for BuyerSeat status: pending/approved/rejected/ready_to_serve/active/complete), full Deal object spec (terms, inventory, curation), seller status management (active/paused/pending/archived). Maps our DealOutput model to IAB Deal object. Depends on 2A (state machine), 1A (FreeWheel).

title: 1I: Buyer Agent Authentication & Access Tiers
description: Authenticate buyer agents and map to access tiers. The 4-tier pricing system exists (Public/Seat/Agency/Advertiser) but has no auth mechanism. API key authentication for known buyer agents. Anonymous access = Public tier (price ranges, limited avails). Agent registration endpoint. Automatic tier assignment based on identity. Optional .well-known/agent.json for agent discovery (AdCP pattern). Lightweight — not full OAuth (that is Phase 3). Creates src/ad_seller/auth/ (api_key_auth.py, agent_registry.py). Modifies api/main.py, settings.py. No blockers.

title: 2A: Order Workflow State Machine
description: Replace linear ExecutionStatus enum with formal state machine: draft > submitted > pending_approval > approved > rejected > in_progress > completed > failed. Configurable transitions with guard conditions. Audit log of all state transitions with timestamps and actor info. Unify ExecutionStatus (flow_state.py:19-34) with ExecutionOrderStatus (core.py:125-132). Depends on 1G (event bus for approval states).

title: 1A: FreeWheel Ad Server Integration
description: FreeWheel ad server client for NBCU pilot. Integration method TBD (REST API or MCP). Covers: IO creation, campaign/placement management, deal ID registration, inventory sync, audience segments. Implements the AdServerClient interface from 1D. BLOCKED until FreeWheel API/MCP access is available. Builds on existing stubs in execution_activation_flow.py:155-189 and settings.py:69-70.

title: 1C: FreeWheel CrewAI Tools
description: CrewAI tools for FreeWheel operations following the pattern in tools/gam/. Tools: create_order, create_campaign, create_deal, list_ad_slots, sync_inventory, book_deal. Creates src/ad_seller/tools/freewheel/. Depends on 1A, 1B.

title: 1D: Ad Server Abstraction Layer
description: Abstract AdServerClient interface that GAM and FreeWheel both implement. Factory selects based on config. Defines the contract FreeWheel must satisfy regardless of API or MCP protocol. Extract interface from existing GAM client. Creates src/ad_seller/clients/ad_server_base.py. Modifies execution_activation_flow.py to replace if/elif branching with polymorphic dispatch. No blockers — start immediately.
close_reason: Implemented ad server abstraction layer: AdServerClient abstract interface with normalized models (AdServerOrder, AdServerLineItem, AdServerDeal, etc.), GAMAdServerClient adapter wrapping existing SOAP+REST clients, factory function get_ad_server_client(), and updated ExecutionActivationFlow to use polymorphic dispatch.

title: 1G: Human-in-the-Loop Event Bus
description: Reusable async pub/sub messaging system for human-in-the-loop tasks throughout the workflow. NOT just approval hooks — a general-purpose event bus that developers can connect to webhooks, SQS queues, CRM systems, Slack, or any external system. Supports: publish events (approval needed, review needed, finance sign-off), subscribe to events (webhook, SQS, pubsubhubbub), respond to events (approve/reject/comment via API). Use cases: deal approval above threshold, creative review, finance budget approval, ad ops team review, any async human task in the adops workflow. Creates src/ad_seller/events/ (event_bus.py, subscribers.py, webhook_handler.py). Modifies proposal_handling_flow.py, deal_generation_flow.py, api/main.py. No blockers.
close_reason: Implemented event bus with InMemoryEventBus (dev) and StorageEventBus (SQLite/Redis), ApprovalGate for human-in-the-loop approval, event emission in proposal/deal/execution flows, 6 new API endpoints for events and approvals, and configurable approval gates behind feature flags.

title: 2C: Change Request Management
description: Post-deal modifications: flight date changes, impression adjustments, price mods, creative swaps. Change request model with diff tracking (what changed, who, when). Re-validation pipeline triggering pricing/availability/approval checks. Ad server update propagation to FreeWheel/GAM. Maps to PRD UpdateCampaign, CreateNewProduct, ReviseCreative. Depends on 2A (state machine).

title: 1B: FreeWheel Data Models
description: Pydantic models for FreeWheel entities parallel to gam.py (479 lines). Mapping: GAM Order = FW Insertion Order, GAM LineItem = FW Campaign+Placement, GAM AdUnit = FW Site>Section>AdSlot, GAM PrivateAuction = FW Programmatic Deal. Creates src/ad_seller/models/freewheel.py. Depends on 1A.

title: 3B: Builder Guides for Vertical Customization
description:
# 3B: Builder Guides for Vertical Customization

## Overview
Scenario-driven guides for teams customizing the seller agent for their specific business. Each guide walks through a real vertical with concrete examples of what to change and where.

## Problem
The platform is highly configurable but it's not obvious how to:
- Customize pricing models and discount tiers
- Update prompts in sub-agents for domain-specific negotiation
- Add business-specific "skills" (tools) to agents
- Enforce compliance policies (HIPAA, political ad rules, etc.)
- Configure inventory types and deal structures for different media

## Planned Guides

### Guide 1: Healthcare Publisher (e.g., WebMD)
- HIPAA privacy skills that prevent prohibited targeting
- Buyer vetting and restricted advertiser categories
- Custom pricing for pharma vs. general advertisers
- Compliance agent that validates proposals against regulations

### Guide 2: CTV/Streaming Publisher (e.g., political ad rules)
- Political advertising policies and seasonal overrides
- Linear + CTV unified inventory setup
- FreeWheel integration specifics
- Content adjacency and brand safety rules

### Guide 3: News/Media Publisher (general web)
- High-volume programmatic with floor price management
- Category blocking and sensitive content policies
- Header bidding integration alongside direct deals
- Seasonal pricing (elections, holidays, tentpole events)

### Guide 4: Custom Pricing & Negotiation
- How to modify pricing rules engine tiers and volume curves
- Updating negotiation agent prompts for different strategies
- Adding custom tools (e.g., competitive rate lookup, audience valuation)
- Configuring approval gates for high-value deals

## Each Guide Covers
1. **Business context** — who this is for, what makes them different
2. **Configuration changes** — settings.py, pricing tiers, feature flags
3. **Agent customization** — prompt modifications, new tools, skill additions
4. **Policy enforcement** — how to add compliance rules that can't be overridden
5. **Testing** — how to validate customizations work correctly

## Timing
Best written after Phase 1 core is stable. Depends on having the full feature set available so guides aren't speculative.

title: 3C: Deployment & Operations Guide
description:
# 3C: Deployment & Operations Guide

## Overview
Production deployment and day-2 operations documentation. Pairs with 3G (IaC) to provide both the automation and the human-readable runbook.

## Sections
1. **Quickstart** — Local development setup, env vars, first API call
2. **Deployment Options** — Docker, AWS (CloudFormation), Terraform, bare metal
3. **Storage Configuration** — SQLite (dev) vs Redis (prod), migration path
4. **Monitoring & Observability** — Event bus as audit log, key metrics, alerting
5. **Scaling** — When to move from SQLite to Redis, horizontal scaling considerations
6. **Security** — API authentication, buyer identity verification, secrets management
7. **Troubleshooting** — Common issues, event bus diagnostics, approval timeout handling

## Dependencies
- Benefits from 3G (IaC) being complete for deployment automation sections
- Benefits from 3A (API Reference) for cross-linking

title: 3A: API & Architecture Reference Documentation
description:
# 3A: API & Architecture Reference Documentation

## Overview
Living documentation that gets updated incrementally as each feature lands. This is the technical reference for developers integrating with or extending the seller agent.

## Sections
1. **Architecture Overview** — System diagram, flow lifecycle, agent topology
2. **REST API Reference** — All endpoints (products, pricing, proposals, deals, events, approvals), request/response schemas, error codes
3. **Configuration Reference** — All settings.py options with defaults and env var names
4. **Storage Schema** — Key patterns, data models, backend options (SQLite/Redis)
5. **Event Bus & Approval Gates** — Event types, approval workflow, resume pattern
6. **Agent & Crew Reference** — Each CrewAI agent's role, tools, and prompt structure
7. **Extension Points** — Where to add custom tools, agents, pricing rules, ad server adapters

## Approach
- Created as a docs/ directory with markdown files
- Updated incrementally as each Phase 1/2 feature lands
- Each feature PR should include a docs update (even if just a new endpoint added to the API ref)

## Deliverables
- docs/architecture.md
- docs/api-reference.md
- docs/configuration.md
- docs/storage.md
- docs/events-and-approvals.md
- docs/agents.md
- docs/extending.md

title: 1F: Multi-turn Session Persistence
description: Persist conversation state across hours/days so buyer agents can resume negotiations. Session model with session_id, conversation_history, current_proposal, negotiation_state. Currently chat history is in-memory only. Modifies chat/main.py, storage/base.py, api/main.py. Creates src/ad_seller/models/session.py. No blockers.

title: 1E: Linear TV Specialist Agent
description: Full linear TV channel specialist for NBCU. Dayparts, scatter/upfront deals, commercial pod positions, GRP-based buying, audience guarantees. Products: NBC Primetime :30, Bravo Reality :15, Telemundo :30. Creates linear_tv_inventory_agent.py. Modifies flow_state.py (add linear_tv type), product_setup_flow.py (NBCU products), inventory_crews.py. Maps to PRD NBC inputs: GetProducts, GetTargeting, GetRate, GetAvails. No blockers.
